reviews.review_description:
This is a great follow up to Wrong Voyage EX. This dev is knocking it out of the park as far as artistry and originality is concerned. Offering low cost inventive first person shooters is a recipe for success. I hope the dev continues with more fun and creative games that I can buy. 
This is a a helluva trip with a cool ending.

developer_response.body: thank you very much for the feedback and really glad you enjoy my little games. I'll try to keep developing rare things like this while there are users who enjoy them. Thanks again.

reviews.review_title: Another great game from the developer
reviews.review_description: Wrong voyage ( the developers other title)it’s in general my most favorite game for go so I was very curious about oxygen dreams and I did. It got disappointed ! It’s a vr platform game which is first person so it needs some vr legs . If you have them get ready for a very addictive game !!!

I love your two games. I bought for 2 euros on the sale, I think it's the right price given that it's somewhat short, but everything is very well made and gives you a memorable experience. Great for people who likes adventures and retro games.
Another really charming, gentle FPS explorer from the maker of wrong voyage.

I love jumper in ego perspective. Good atmosphere but the controls are a bit confusing in the beginning. Gamepad Support would be 5 stars from me

Edit: thx for your quick response. Walking with gamepad and aiming with gear controller would be perfect! :)

developer_response.body: thank you very much for the review. I'm thinking about supporting gamepad in one hand to move and strafe, and the Go/ GearVR controller to aim and shoot, but i can't promise anything. Thanks again.

reviews.review_description: Why cant we use the touch pad as a joystick? We can only move forward, not backward and sideways. The direction I am looking should be forward always. Something that you can easily do in "Daedalus" and all VR games to this day. This makes even the simplest movement and platform like feels like the most difficult challenge ever made in a videogame. Would give 5 stars and recommend it to my friends if only I could play it with an XBox controller : aim to shoot with my head and jump and move with the controller.

developer_response.body: thanks for the feedback and really sorry to hear so many problems. Maybe controls explanation is not clear enough. You can move in the direction the controller (your hand) is pointing. This way you can strafe pointing left or right with your hand, and swipe with your finger on the touchpad to rotate, or even rotate with your own body if you are playing on a swivel chair or standing. I know that controls look very different at first, but i think they work well once you get used to them. Please, give the game another chance.

reviews.review_description: Go controller does not work well. Have to look straight up press the trigger just to jump. Maybe a controller option?

developer_response.body: thanks for the feedback. I'm not sure if controls explanation in the opening scene is clear enough... you don't need to look up to jump, just pointing with the controller up or down. I think once you get used to the controls they work well.

reviews.review_description:
This is a pretty fun vr platformer with crisp graphics and a thorough style. But the control scheme is weird. It is like you are strapped to a rocket and can only change direction by pointing it in a different direction. I would prefer strafing like in a fps at least as long the player moves on the ground. 

Otherwise fun game.
What a blast! Great game, nice artstyle and well designed controls and levels. You'll feel like Link in a Zelda dungeon from time to time.

reviews.review_title: Worthy follow up to Wrong Voyage Ex
reviews.review_description: It’s clear by now that fguillotine is a gifted game developer with a signature style all his own. The intentionally low-poly graphics, the retro 8-bit music, the brilliant platforming, odd creatures in an odd world, the imaginative level designs, and the always suspenseful tone, are the hallmarks of his projects. Oxygen Dreams is the work of a born creator and, after the truly splendid Wrong Voyage Ex, it solidifies his place in the VR world. Allow me to first explain why it does not achieve 5-star status. While this title is its own entity, it does often feel like a spiritual sequel to that game. At times, you will feel like you are perhaps playing an expansion pack or an outtake of it, because it doesn’t really distinguish itself too much artistically. The story, to the extent there is one, is confusing and inconsequential. I’m still not really sure why my character was doing any of this, other than it was just a dream. Ok, so the developer isn’t much of a storyteller; that is fine. Just because this game doesn’t match the overall brilliance of its predecessor does not lessen its standing in the hierarchy of must-have VR titles. It’s still better than a great deal of product out there. Even if you have not played W.V.Ex, you will surely enjoy this game immensely. If you have, then you will be delighted to know that the developer has not lost a beat, and has continued to produce an experience that is true to his strengths. Really, that’s all we ever need out of the best game makers.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your reviews. Both are awesome and 
they encourage me to keep developing these little games. I'm trying to do something different for the next one... hope you like it too.

reviews.review_description: So this game is fun but it has tons of platforming and people know that in large the oculus controller is hard to pull off and when ppl see no controller support they tend to turn away being able to put gamepad under controller options would help because its a good game and i want more ppl to see it so hopefully you add gamepad support nobody should design platforming games without a gamepad in mind it just doesnt work i can list the action/platforming that work well with the oculus controller on 1 hand honestly and most devs should know this after a year but its still a fun game it is just frusturaring how it is now PLZ WE STILL NEED CONTROLLER SUPPORT or MID LVL SAVES plz plz plz i want to play this game more really bad but with clunky control and no mid lvl save system your gonna get bummed out fast such a shame but im keeping it at 5 stars for now

It took me a while to figure out the nonstandard control scheme... 
SHOOT: Point the controller at the target and pull trigger.
JUMP: Point controller straight upwards (or down) and pull trigger.
MOVE: Point controller in direction you want to go and press touch pad. Point forward to go forward, left/right to strafe, point the controller behind you to move backwards, etc.
Once you learn these controls, things start to click.

Of course, at any given time these three possible actions give you three different directions you might want to point the controller. There's a lot of rapid pointing in this game, it's used for lots of things.

The game can be frustrating at times, since any mistake resets the level. I sometimes wish that you were reset to the last switch you flipped rather than the beginning. There were some 'Not again!' moments. But once I got the hang of it, it's definitely fun.

The graphics are cute and well done. Voxel goodness! The game runs really smoothly on go. The music is good.

This games small file size is a real selling point: well under 200MB. If you're GO is filling up, and you want something small in file size but big in scope, this could be a really fun option.

developer_response.body: thanks a lot for the long and detailed review, and glad you like the game. I know controls look very different at first..., i'm planning to add a more clear tutorial or training room to explain them better. About the checkpoints, i didn't think they could be needed, cause once you get used to the controls, you can go through each level in a few minutes, but i will consider to include them too. Thanks again

reviews.review_description: The game itself is amazing. But it is very annoying that you are forced to replay the whole level from the beginning again and again just because the player character sometimes randomly doesn't jump when you want it to, so he falls down and dies, I'm so angry because of this. Basically this part of gameplay ruined such an atmospheric game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback. Once you get used to the controls you can go through almost every level in less than five minutes, but i know it's not easy at first. It's a game where you need to practice to become better, like any classic platform game. Once you get used to the game mechanics and rules things will turn easier, although i know the game is not for everyone. Thanks again.
